meaning: n.灯火管制,(暂时的)眩晕,中断
examples: She had a blackout and couldn't remember anything about the accident.
As the blackout hour moved across the globe, London's Big Ben, Paris' Eiffel Tower, and Arc de Triomphe flipped the switch.
The blackout poems were a side project.
There are no blackout dates or restrictions of any kind when using your rewards points.
Syrian residents confirmed the blackout, but said mobile phones and landlines were working normally.
